GDoll's damage amp doesn't really affect Mouchette

...her ult gives 100% FINAL damage, it's absurd

Mouch doesn't care about damage amp

The majority of the damage from Mouchette comes from the fact that she uses a steroid for her strike damage that isn't affected by base damage; if you get 100% damage amp on Mouchette, your Strike gains 39 damage or some shit
and your strike does 1000 damage because Shining Tornado gives it +485 and another hit count

Helot gets recommended over characters like Arachne or GDoll because her exalt gives *final* damage, which is multiplicative with other forms of boost that don't work with amp. Notably, damage amp doesn't affect the increase in damage you get from Strength, either.

Mouchette feels like she ramps up super hard because she gets +25% final damage to her strikes every time she fires her Pursuit off, so you play everyone else's strikes, mortal blast a few times, and finish with Shining Crush. GDoll is kind of a nonfactor in that scenario.
although GDoll will amp the pursuits, which hurt a lot.
Tldr don't use gdoll

Arachne's still better than GDoll because if the Singularity thing on the cards being final damage, mind, it's absolutely crazy, like just silly, it's just Helot's easier and you're more likely to have the copies on hand.

well because you need to trigger DR before the damage is in play. it depends what your trying to do. if you scale heavy on dr with committed covs and WoD. your damage at the start will not be comparable to a build with damage already built in the cov and units that support a front up fight. 

there are pro's and cons. 
you might just not have enough damage to push to the phases and your DR gets wasted early for example.
but if you push a standard dps build with damage already built in the WoD or cov like crit damage. it becomes easier to guage but your ability to pump the passive damage jump via dr plummets. It's also more resource intensive to centralize your build on DR. it has the ability to "cheese" 1 hit bosses while pumping damage. Dr builds also dont have Crit rate problems after a certain amount of WoD and threshold. 

2x 100% DR cov = 30% crit rate and crit damage while having 200%DR for the passive to use vs 1 titan at +12 is 28% crit rate and 60% crit damage after exalting but you miss out on the DR.
it just becomes a math thing for you and where you are in the game
also what your planned cov/unit comp etc is.

ive played with her a bit and if your planning to do a titans build then its easier to do and she will play well with saya but if you want a DR build you will probably need a plan when doing pair ups with units like feint/horla(no access to helot) etc.
